title: Copying boards/automations across accounts
excerpt:
Description
Most automations copy over fine but when the automation requires a particular user or email address - to notify someone for example, it doesn’t copy over.  It’s easy to forget you had that automation in the o…

posts.author: Carrie (Carrie Downey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Most automations copy over fine but when the automation requires a particular user or email address - to notify someone for example, it doesn’t copy over. It’s easy to forget you had that automation in the originating location. It would be helpful if the automation copied over as a placeholder (grayed out) even if the specifications needed reset once it arrives to the destination account. It’s easy to forget what automations are missing otherwise.

What are you trying to achieve

Saving time cross referencing every automation recipe after a cross account copy took place.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 this is super frustrating.

It’s not just that it doesn’t copy cleanly, it actually breaks the automation by dropping the user/email pieces entirely. Notifications, email templates, recipients… just gone with no clear indication.

That’s the worst part, you think everything copied fine until something doesn’t fire.

Having placeholders or even a “needs reconfiguration” flag would make a huge difference:

  • keeps the structure intact

  • makes it obvious what’s missing

  • prevents silent failures

Right now it’s way too easy to miss and ends up causing downstream issues.
